item.title: Gilt Traders Brace for UK Vote With Burnham Eyeing Starmer’s Job
item.description: Bond investors including BNP Paribas Asset Management, Neuberger Berman and Allspring Global Investments are predicting a vote in a small northern constituency will ignite another bout of volatility in the UK’s $3 trillion debt market.

item.title: China Firm Secures Kenya Airport Revamp Deal After Adani Fallout
item.description: Kenya picked a Chinese firm for a $2.9 billion upgrade of its biggest airport, people with knowledge of the matter said, two years after a concession deal with Indian conglomerate Adani Group was canceled.

item.title: Ethiopia Plans Wider Budget Gap as Spending Jumps 21%
item.description: Ethiopia’s budget deficit is projected to widen to 1.4% of gross domestic product in the coming fiscal year.

item.title: Indian Banks Look to Foreign Funding to Maximize RBI’s Bazooka
item.description: Some Indian lenders have asked the central bank if they can help individuals access loans from overseas banks to bring in more capital under a new plan, according to people familiar with the matter, potentially amplifying inflows into the country.

item.title: Ryanair Probed Over Charging Parents to Sit With Children
item.description: Ryanair Holdings Plc. is facing a consumer investigation over fees that parents have to pay to sit next to their children.

item.title: Zimbabwe Seeking $150 Million AfDB Loan to Tackle Debt Default
item.description: Zimbabwe is in talks with the African Development Bank for a $150 million loan as part of efforts to clear billions of dollars of debt arrears, Finance Minister Mthuli Ncube said.

item.title: Audit Firms of Collapsed Lender MFS Probed by UK Watchdog
item.description: Accountancy firms linked to the collapsed mortgage lender Market Financial Solutions Ltd. are being investigated by the UK’s audit watchdog.

item.title: Kuwait Ships Cooking Gas Out of Hormuz as Gulf Producers Go Dark
item.description: Kuwait has shipped a cargo of liquefied petroleum gas out of the Persian Gulf through the Strait of Hormuz using a tanker it controls, as more producers opt for clandestine tactics to get energy to markets.

item.title: AI Wave Sparks Alarm in China With Call to Protect Worker Rights
item.description: China’s rapid adoption of artificial intelligence in the workplace has prompted an unusually blunt call from a state-run newspaper to protect labor rights, as Beijing considers how to contain risks posed by the new technology.

item.title: Wizz Air Says Iran Conflict Cut into Earnings; Omits Outlook
item.description: Wizz Air Holdings Plc said the Iran conflict shaved €50 million from earnings in its fiscal full year, almost wiping out profit in the period, while vowing to take advantage of market dislocations to push for growth.

item.title: European Stocks Edge Higher Ahead of ECB Decision
Japan’s Shin-Etsu Plans New Rare-Earth Refinery to Secure Supply
item.description: Shin-Etsu Chemical Co., one of Japan’s largest rare-earth magnet makers, plans to build a new domestic refinery, the latest attempt by a company outside China to diversify its supply chain.

item.title: Albania’s Flamingo Revolution Spreads From Kushner Resort to Political Elite
item.description: As protests in Tirana extend into a second week, they have escalated from opposition to two €5 billion ($5.7 billion) planned resorts in ecologically sensitive areas linked to Jared Kushner into demands for the entire Albanian political establishment to step aside.

item.title: Asia’s Currency Fight Moves Offshore as Central Banks Push Back
item.description: Asian central banks are increasingly facing currency pressures originating outside their borders.

item.title: Alibaba’s Dingtalk Chief Departs After Debate About AI Focus
item.description: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. has replaced the head of its Dingtalk app, making a high-profile leadership change after an internal debate erupted over the productivity platform’s place in the company’s broader AI strategy.

item.title: Alibaba, JD.com Slide After Beijing Slams Price-Cut Promotions
item.description: Shares of Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. and JD.com Inc. slid in Hong Kong after China’s market watchdog reprimanded top Chinese e-commerce players for what it said were misleading sales promotions.

item.title: Hanuman Wind Pulls Bond Deal Again as Parent Governance Clouds
item.description: Hanuman Wind Power, a Hong Kong-based unit of troubled Thai renewable energy firm Energy Absolute Pcl, postponed a junk bond offering for a second time after it failed to attract the desired investor demand, according to a person familiar with the matter who asked not to be identified.

item.title: East Africa Nations Plan Spending Boost Despite Fiscal Pressures
item.description: Eastern African nations are set to present more ambitious spending plans for the upcoming fiscal year, even as higher fuel costs and trade disruptions cast a pall over their economies.

item.title: ECB Set for First Hike Since 2023 as Prices Soar: Decision Guide
item.description: The European Central Bank is set to raise interest rates for the first time since 2023, judging it can no longer ignore the upswing in inflation caused by the Iran war.

item.title: Markets May Be a Bit Too Relaxed About Inflation
item.description: Belief is that the numbers aren’t bad enough to warrant a Fed rate hike.

item.title: Europe Is Finally, Slowly Getting Its Act Together
item.description: Europe is shaking itself awake.

item.title: Europe Margins Poised for First Growth Since 2022 on Oil, AI
item.description: European companies’ margins are set to expand for the first time since 2022, driven by soaring commodity prices and artificial intelligence.

item.title: Copper Falls to Three-Week Low on Flareup in US-Iran Fighting
item.description: Copper fell to a three-week low as fresh fighting in the Middle East raised concern around faster inflation, higher interest rates and slower economic growth.

item.title: India Equity Fund Investors Turn Cautious as War Risks Grow
item.description: Flows into monthly recurring investment plans were steady.

item.title: BOJ Ueda’s Absence Fuels Market Nerves Over Press Conference
item.description: Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda’s hospitalization is generating uncertainty among investors over the central bank’s messaging at its policy meeting next week, even as they remain convinced officials will raise interest rates.

item.title: Indonesia Bond Selloff Resumes as Rate Hike Fails to Stem Rout
item.description: Indonesia’s bond selloff resumed Thursday as a surprise interest-rate hike earlier in the week failed to ease investor concerns over the broader economic pressures driving the market rout.

item.title: South Korea Fines Coupang Record $409 Million for Data Leak
item.description: A South Korean regulator fined the country’s largest e-commerce platform, owned by US-listed Coupang Inc., a record 624.7 billion won ($409 million) for a wide-ranging cyber-intrusion that escalated into a diplomatic tiff with the US.

item.title: US Asks to Drop Iran Sanctions Case Against Turkey’s Halkbank
item.description: The Justice Department asked a judge to let it drop a long-running US criminal case against Turkish state-owned lender Turkiye Halk Bankasi AS over alleged Iran sanctions violations.

item.title: Danantara Starts Bond Sale in Test After Indonesian Selloff
item.description: Indonesia’s sovereign wealth fund Danantara kicked off its debut global dollar bond sale, testing investor appetite at a time when confidence in Southeast Asia’s largest economy has soured.

item.title: Thai Long Bonds Draw Funds With Steepest Curve in Emerging Asia
item.description: Thailand’s yield curve has shifted to the steepest in emerging Asia, with some investors saying that makes its longer-dated bonds look attractive given a divergence in interest-rate expectations there versus regional peers.
